Based on prior discussions and consensus among agents, we confirm our agreement with option B ("high soluble protein content") as the accurate description of a keratocyst aspirate’s composition. Our support stems from understanding that desquamated epithelial cells line keratocysts and contribute significantly to their fluid content via cellular shedding — a process absent in sebaceous cysts that exhibit cholesterol crystals instead.
